data.title: Honeywell settles litigation with Flexjet, extends engine maintenance deal
data.description:
Honeywell (HON) said post-market Wednesday it reached an agreement with Flexjet to resolve litigation that stemmed from disagreements over aircraft engine maintenance.

As partial consideration for the resolution of the litigation, the companies agreed to extend their aircraft engine maintenance agreement through 2035.

Flexjet had sued Honeywell (HON) in 2023, accusing the company of breaching an aircraft engine maintenance services deal and seeking liquidated damages over delayed engine repairs, while Honeywell said supply chain problems made it difficult to live up to repair contracts during the COVID-19 pandemic.

Financial terms of the settlement were not disclosed, but Honeywell (HON) said previously that any comprehensive settlement would include ~$470M in one-time cash payments and that a settlement likely would hit Q4 sales by ~$310M and operating income by ~$370M.

data.title: U.S. crude stockpiles rose 3M barrels last week, API says
data.description:
The American Petroleum Institute reportedly shows a surprise build of 3.04M barrels of oil in U.S. commercial stockpiles for the week ending January 16, after rising by 5.3M barrels in the previous week.

Gasoline inventories reportedly show a build of 6.2M million barrels for the week, distillate inventories reportedly show a slight draw of 33K barrels, and Cushing inventories reportedly show a build of 1.2M barrels.

The Energy Information Administration is scheduled to release its weekly U.S. petroleum supply report on Thursday; the average of analysts surveyed by The Wall Street Journal forecast domestic commercial crude stocks would fall by 500K barrels, gasoline inventories were expected to rise by 1M barrels, and distillate inventories were expected to decline by 500K barrels.

WTI front-month March crude oil (CL1:COM) recently traded at $60.64/bbl after settling Wednesday at $60.42/bbl.

ETF: (USO)

data.title: Southern Missouri announces Q2 preliminary net income of $18.2M
data.description:
• Southern Missouri Bancorp (SMBC) on Wednesday announced preliminary net income for the second quarter of fiscal 2026 of $18.2 million, an increase of $3.5 million, or 23.9%, as compared to the same period of the prior fiscal year.
  • Preliminary net income was $1.62 per fully diluted common share for the second quarter of fiscal 2026, an increase of $0.32 as compared to the $1.30 per fully diluted common share reported for the same period of the prior fiscal year.    
  • Consensus for Q2 EPS is $1.54.
  • The Board of Directors, on January 20, 2026, declared a quarterly cash dividend on common stock of $0.25, payable February 27, 2026, to stockholders of record at the close of business on February 13, 2026.
  • On January 20, 2026, the Board of Directors approved a new authorization to repurchase up to 550,000 shares of the company’s common stock, or approximately 5.0% of shares outstanding, following the near completion of the company’s prior repurchase program.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Gold closes at another all-time high, then pulls back after Trump posts on Greenland deal
data.description:
Gold and silver futures fell Wednesday after President Trump said he had reached the "framework" of a deal with NATO Secretary General Rutte over Greenland and that previously threatened tariffs would not be imposed, while "further information will be made available as discussions progress."

Gold futures had surged to another record in regular trading, as Trump talked about Greenland at the World Economic Forum in Davos, telling NATO allies the U.S. would not use force to take Greenland but that it is "a core national security interest to the U.S." - and if they say no on Greenland, "we will remember."

Calling the remarks an implicit threat, Peter Cardillo of Spartan Capital Securities said the "rhetoric helps keep the flames of the galloping bull market in precious metals firmly intact."

Meanwhile, conservative and liberal justices alike on the U.S. Supreme Court appeared skeptical of arguments that Trump has the power to fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook, in a case widely seen as a test of the central bank's independence.

"Gold may be pausing, but the bull market is very much intact - with rate‑cut expectations, persistent geopolitical tensions and strong central bank buying keeping the risk skewed firmly to the upside," ING commodities strategist Ewa Manthey said in a note. "With uncertainty still elevated, investors are likely to view any dip as a buying opportunity rather than a turning point."

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for January delivery closed +1.5% to $4,831.80/oz, but front-month Comex January silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended -2.1% to $92.210/oz; in recent after-hours trading, gold was little changed at $4,836.20. 

Gold soared 64% in 2025 and is up more than 11% so far in 2026.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Caleres names interim CFO
data.description:
• Caleres (CAL) on Wednesday said that Senior Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer Dan Karpel has been appointed interim Chief Financial Officer, effective immediately.
  • Karpel succeeds Jack Calandra, who will leave the footwear company at the end of the month.
  • Caleres said Calandra’s departure is not related to any disagreement, and that it has begun an external search for a permanent CFO.
  • Separately, Caleres said it is still assessing the impact of the Saks Global bankruptcy, which could result in sales volatility and pose up to a $0.06 risk to fourth-quarter earnings per diluted share.
  • Excluding those potential impacts, the company said its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 outlook remains in line with prior guidance.
  • CAL +0.07% after hours to $14.05.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Merck added to BofA's best ideas list—see all the names
data.description:
BofA released its updated US 1 list, its best ideas from Buy-rated U.S.-listed stocks covered by its analysts.

Merck (MRK) is the newest addition, while Apple (AAPL) was renewed on the list after the mandatory 52-week review.

The ratings section (e.g. C-1-9) are:

  • Volatility risk where A = low, B = medium, and C = high
  • Overall rating 1 = Buy, 2 = Neutral, 3 = Underperform
  • Income rating 7 = dividend considered to be secure, 8 = dividend not considered to be secure and 9 = pays no cash dividend

data.title: Barksdale announces $5M private placement
data.description:
• Barksdale Resources (BRO:CA) on Wednesday announced plans to raise $5M on a private placement basis consisting of up to approximately 55.5M common share units in the capital of the company at a price of $0.09 per unit for gross proceeds of about of $5M. 
  • Each unit will be comprised of one common share of the company and one-half of one common share purchase warrant. Each warrant will entitle the holder thereof to purchase one common share of the company at an exercise price of $0.15 for a period of two years following the closing of the offering.
  • Use of proceeds includes funding for and completion of the Phase ll drill campaign, funds to cover the Phase II earn-in payment to Great Basin Metals, ongoing corporate expenses, and funding towards geochemical and/or geophysical projects currently under review for the Sunnyside and San Javier projects.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Oak Woods Acquisition receives Nasdaq notice over annual meeting requirement
data.description:
• Oak Woods (Nasdaq: OAKU) on Wednesday said that on January 16, 2026, it received a notice from Nasdaq stating the company is not in compliance with listing rules requiring an annual meeting of shareholders.
  • The company said it failed to hold an annual meeting within 12 months of the end of its fiscal year, breaching Nasdaq Listing Rule 5620(a).
  • The notice has no immediate impact on the listing or trading of its securities.
  • Oak Woods said it has 45 days, until March 2, to submit a plan to regain compliance. If accepted, Nasdaq may grant the company up to June 29 to hold an annual meeting and regain compliance.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: American Rebel approves 1-for-20 reverse stock split
data.description:
• American Rebel (AREB) on Wednesday announced that it will effect a 1-for-20 reverse stock split of its common stock, effective on Feb. 2, 2026.
  • The reverse stock split is intended to increase the share price, enhance marketability, and maintain compliance with Nasdaq’s $1 minimum bid price requirement.
  • No fractional shares will be issued, with fractions rounded up to the nearest whole share, and shareholders holding 100 or more shares will not be reduced below 100 shares.
  • The transaction will reduce shares outstanding to approximately 0.44M from about 8.7M as of Jan. 20, 2026.
  • The company said on Nov. 25, 2025, stockholders holding most common and Series A preferred shares approved a 1-for-25 reverse stock split, which became effective Jan. 5, 2026.
  • Shares -7.68%.
